rule_description: The governed signals dataset must contain at least one row.
All required signal columns (drift_event_id, record_hash, calculation_run_id, forecast_key, forecast_version, forecast_drift_score, drift_status, is_event, etc.) are present.
No duplicate rows on the governed grain (calculation_version, scenario, forecast_key, forecast_version, drift_type).
Every record_hash value is unique across the signals dataset.
Performance/Shape/Stability/Volatility/composite drift scores and score_coverage_pct lie within [0, 100].
The composite forecast_drift_score is non-null for every signal.
The governed family weights (Performance 20 + Shape 40 + Stability 30 + Volatility 10) sum to 100.
No infinite (Inf) values exist in the numeric family-score metrics.
Every signal has a non-empty forecast_key.
Each signal has exactly four family-score rows (Performance, Shape, Stability, Volatility).
confidence_level is one of {HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW}.
drift_status is one of {Healthy, Watch, Warning, Critical, Unknown}.
expected_value: All values in {Healthy, Watch, Warning, Critical, Unknown}

rule_description: The forecast_key_raw lineage column (pre-canonicalization) is present (E5B I1).
forecast_key equals its canonical form UPPER(TRIM(forecast_key)) for every row.
severity is populated only where is_event = 1; otherwise it is null.
Family rows with eligibility_status = NOT_COMPUTABLE have a null family_score (never treated as zero).
